Description:en: Local care for wounds, infected and non-infected.
A closure by suture is when stitches, or sutures, are used to close a variety of wound types. The skin is sewn together in order to heal.
Is a minor surgical procedure used to treat a localized collection of pus, known as an abscess.
Catheterization of the urinary bladder is a medical procedure in which a sterile tube (urinary catheter) is inserted through the urethra into the bladder to drain urine.

Insertion of an intravenous (IV) line is a clinical procedure in which a sterile cannula (small tube) is inserted into a vein to administer fluids, medications, or blood products directly into the bloodstream.
Nebulization is a medical procedure in which liquid medication is converted into a fine mist (aerosol) using a nebulizer machine, allowing the patient to inhale the drug directly into the lungs.
Debridement of a skin wound is a medical procedure involving the removal of dead (necrotic), infected, or contaminated tissue from a wound to promote healing and prevent infection.
Injection administration is the process of delivering medication into the body using a needle and syringe. It allows for faster absorption and precise dosing compared to oral medications.
Splint application is the procedure of applying a rigid or semi-rigid device to an injured body part to immobilize and support bones, joints, or soft tissues, preventing movement and further injury.
This is a procedure of inserting a contraceptive implant to prevent conception.
This is a procedure of removing contraceptive implant.
Is the process of removing dirt, dust, organic matter, and impurities from surfaces, objects, or environments.

Fully specified name:en: REMOVAL OF SKIN SUTURES OR CLIPS
Description:en: Is the process of taking out stitches (sutures) or surgical clips from a healed wound or surgical incision.
